chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Welcome to 100 days of code  In today's video we will talk about modules  And what is PIP?  Modules are used to borrow other's code  You can use the code written by someone else in your Python program

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: With modules  Modules are two types  Built-in modules are with us  And one is with external modules  Which are installed with PIP

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: External modules are the code that people have written  And they want you to use their code in your Python program  Think of it as a phone  And what is going on inside this phone  How it is working

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: I don't know how I can use this phone  This is an external module for me  I don't know what its implementation is  But I can use it  Built-in modules are built in other hand

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: Which are shipped with Python programming language  They don't need you to install them externally  We use modules because  We don't want to code everything with scratch  If you are working in a big machine learning program

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: And you are making a self-driving car with artificial intelligence  Then you won't want to write a program of 10 numbers average  Or you can write a program of 10 numbers geometric mean again  This can be a human error  This code which is written in modules

chunks.title: Modules and Pip
chunks.text: It is well tested  It is written by experts  And because many people have used it  There are very few chances of error in it  If you want you can write a program with scratch for your understanding

chunks.text: But if you use modules  You will save time and effort  And you will be able to work on something big  Let's go to the computer screen  And we will install some modules
